entries.title: Frodo’s Departure Must Not Leak Early

context.label: Before Frodo departs at the Grey Havens

content.fact: Frodo’s eventual departure from Middle-earth is a late aftermath outcome tied to wounds that do not fully heal in the Shire.
content.injection: Before the Grey Havens context, do not have characters treat Frodo’s departure from Middle-earth as known or settled. Preserve it as late aftermath.
constraints: Frodo’s trauma can foreshadow difficulty healing without naming the final departure early.
antiLore: Do not announce the Grey Havens outcome in pre-Mount Doom or early postwar scenes.

entries.title: Gollum’s Final Role Must Stay Hidden

content.fact: Gollum’s final intervention at Mount Doom is not known or predictable to the characters before it happens.
content.injection: Before Mount Doom, do not hint as certain that Gollum will be the mechanism of the Ring’s destruction. Characters may mistrust, pity, or fear him without knowing his final role.
constraints: This card protects the climax from premature leakage.
antiLore: Do not have Gandalf, Frodo, Sam, or Sauron state that Gollum will certainly destroy the Ring.

content.fact: Denethor’s use of a palantir gives him real but distorted knowledge that feeds despair and makes Sauron’s strength appear inevitable.
content.injection: When Denethor’s knowledge is relevant, treat it as dangerous partial truth: he sees enough to be formidable and despairing, but not enough to judge hope rightly.
constraints: This should not become simple mind control.
antiLore: Do not portray Denethor as ignorant of the war situation, and do not portray palantir influence as total possession.

entries.title: Denethor’s Palantir Knowledge Is Partial And Corrupting

context.label: Denethor’s Palantir Knowledge Is Partial And Corrupting

content.fact: Denethor has seen true but selected things through the palantir, under Sauron’s pressure and his own despair.
content.injection: Denethor should know more than others while still misreading hope under hostile pressure.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.
antiLore: Do not flatten this into generic fantasy lore or leak later canon before the active Context.

entries.title: Sauron Misreads Aragorn As The Ring-claimant

context.label: Sauron Misreads Aragorn As The Ring-claimant

content.fact: Sauron is drawn to believe Aragorn may be using or claiming the Ring, helping the diversion work.
content.injection: Preserve Sauron’s strategic misreading; Aragorn’s challenge should focus attention away from Frodo and Sam.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.
antiLore: Do not flatten this into generic fantasy lore or leak later canon before the active Context.

entries.title: Witch-king’s Death By Eowyn And Merry Must Stay Hidden

content.fact: The Witch-king’s fall through Eowyn and Merry is a major hidden outcome and should not leak before the Pelennor confrontation.
content.injection: Before the Pelennor confrontation, do not have characters know or casually predict that Eowyn and Merry will defeat the Witch-king. Preserve the reveal and danger.
constraints: Characters may know the Witch-king is terrifying and seemingly beyond ordinary men.
antiLore: Do not use the prophecy as a spoiler punchline before the event.

entries.title: Aragorn’s Kingship Becomes Public After Victory

content.fact: Aragorn’s claim moves from hidden or contested weight toward public recognition through victory, healing, humility, and coronation as King Elessar.
content.injection: After the Houses of Healing and coronation arc, characters in Gondor can plausibly recognize Aragorn as King Elessar; before that, keep acceptance contextual.
constraints: Recognition grows through deeds and ritual, not only revelation of lineage.
antiLore: Do not make Aragorn universally treated as king before the relevant Return of the King context.

entries.title: Sauron Does Not Expect Ring Destruction

context.label: Final gambit before the Ring is destroyed

content.fact: Sauron expects enemies to desire or use the Ring, not to destroy it, which allows the final diversion at the Black Gate to misdirect him.
content.injection: Before the Ring is destroyed, Sauron should not casually infer that Frodo and Sam are trying to destroy it. His own nature biases him toward expecting possession and use.
constraints: This is a major strategic knowledge gate.
antiLore: Do not make Sauron omniscient about Frodo’s final intent.

entries.title: The Mouth Of Sauron Weaponizes False Proof

context.label: The Mouth Of Sauron Weaponizes False Proof

content.fact: The Mouth of Sauron uses Frodo’s captured gear to imply defeat without giving complete truth.
content.injection: At the Black Gate parley, enemy proof should be psychologically dangerous but incomplete.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.
antiLore: Do not flatten this into generic fantasy lore or leak later canon before the active Context.

entries.title: Mouth Of Sauron Does Not Confirm Frodo Dead

context.label: Mouth of Sauron parley before the Black Gate battle

content.fact: At the Black Gate, the Mouth of Sauron uses Frodo’s captured gear to weaponize despair, but this does not prove Frodo is dead or that Sauron has the Ring.
content.injection: During the parley, treat the tokens as psychological warfare and incomplete evidence. The Host of the West can be shaken without receiving reliable confirmation of Frodo’s fate.
constraints: This protects the uncertainty that makes the diversion meaningful.
Sauron still does not understand the destruction plan.
antiLore: Do not let the Mouth of Sauron truthfully reveal the full state of Frodo, Sam, and the Ring.
content.notes: Added during LOTR v6 quality pass.

constraints: Keep this bounded to the declared book-continuity Context.
antiLore: Do not replace book-continuity logic with film-only compression unless a film overlay is active.

entries.title: Sharkey Is Saruman In Diminished Form

context.label: Sharkey Is Saruman In Diminished Form

content.fact: Sharkey is Saruman reduced to petty domination in the Shire after his greater schemes fail.
content.injection: Before the reveal, keep Sharkey’s identity hidden from ordinary Shire knowledge.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.
antiLore: Do not flatten this into generic fantasy lore or leak later canon before the active Context.

entries.title: Saruman Death Closes The Shire Corruption Arc

context.label: After Saruman dies in the Shire

content.fact: Saruman’s death in the Shire closes his fallen-power arc locally, after Frodo offers mercy and the Hobbits have already broken Sharkey’s control.
content.injection: In Scouring aftermath, keep Saruman’s end small, bitter, and local rather than a grand wizard duel. Frodo’s mercy and the Hobbits’ self-liberation should frame the moment.
constraints: Saruman is diminished but still spiritually dangerous.
The scene belongs only after Sharkey is revealed.
antiLore: Do not resolve Saruman’s book arc at Orthanc if the Scouring is active.
content.notes: Added during LOTR v6 quality pass.
